name: MPU6050
tagline: 6-axis inertial measurement unit combining a 3-axis gyroscope and 3-axis accelerometer over I2C
description: The MPU6050 is a 6-DOF (Degrees of Freedom) IMU that integrates a 3-axis MEMS gyroscope and a 3-axis MEMS accelerometer in a single chip. It communicates via I2C and includes a built-in Digital Motion Processor (DMP) for onboard sensor fusion. Widely used in drones, gesture controllers, self-balancing robots, and air mouse projects.

specs.onboardDMP: Yes — Digital Motion Processor for quaternion output

pinout.name: Aux SCL
pinout.desc: Auxiliary I2C clock for external sensor

pinout.name: Interrupt
pinout.desc: Active-HIGH interrupt output for data ready events

wiringNote: Pull SDA and SCL lines to 3.3V or 5V with 4.7kΩ resistors if not already present on the module. Most breakout boards include these. Use the INT pin when using the DMP for interrupt-driven data collection.
libraryName: MPU6050 by Electronic Cats or I2Cdevlib
libraryInstall: Arduino IDE → Library Manager → Search 'MPU6050' by Electronic Cats → Install. Also install 'I2Cdevlib-Core'.
commonMistakes: Forgetting Wire.begin() before mpu.initialize() → I2C hangs
Not running I2C scanner first to confirm address (0x68 vs 0x69)
Reading raw values and expecting degrees — must convert using scale factor or use DMP
Mounting the sensor with axes misaligned — must account for orientation in firmware
